name: Patlijan Boereg (an Egyptian Eggplant Specialty)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut unpared eggplant into 1/4-inch thick slices or rounds.
2. Salt lightly and let stand approximately half an hour.
3. Pat each slice dry, dust lightly with flour, and fry in hot oil until nicely browned on both sides.
4. Pour off surplus oil, leaving the fried eggplant slices in the bottom of the frying pan.
5. Remove half the fried eggplant to a plate.
6. Beat 2 eggs, add dry cheese and 1 teaspoon minced parsley, and spread over the eggplant slices in the pan.
7. Cover with remaining fried eggplant, and cook over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes.
8. Beat the remaining egg until light and frothy.
9. Lift the cover and pour the beaten egg over the top of the eggplant.
10. Let cook uncovered until set.
11. Garnish with parsley or a sprinkling of lemon juice.
12. Serve hot.
